care.summary: Stunning heart-shaped leaves in vibrant pink, red, white, and green combinations; a seasonal tropical bulb prized for its colorful foliage.
care.light: Bright indirect to partial shade; avoid direct midday sun which scorches leaves
care.water: Keep soil consistently moist but not waterlogged; reduce watering when leaves die back in dormancy
care.soil: Rich, well-draining organic potting mix with peat moss or coco coir
care.humidity: High humidity preferred (60%+); mist leaves or use a pebble tray
care.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer every 2 weeks during active growth; stop when leaves yellow in fall
care.toxicity: Toxic if ingested (calcium oxalate crystals); keep away from pets and children
common_issues: Leaf scorch from direct sun or low humidity
Root rot from overwatering or poorly draining soil
Dormant dieback in winter if temperature drops below 15°C
tips: Plant tubers 5cm deep with eyes facing up when soil temperature reaches 21°C
Lift and store tubers indoors over winter in cold climates
Remove spent leaves to encourage new growth
Pinch off flower buds to direct energy into foliage display
